description: Infrastructure as Code commands for provisioning and managing cloud resources

commands.name: terraform init
commands.summary: Initialize a working directory: download providers and modules

options.description: Upgrade providers and modules to latest allowed versions
Pass backend config values (e.g. for remote state)

examples.description: Initialize with backend config from a file

commands.name: terraform plan
commands.summary: Preview what changes will be made before applying

options.description: Save the plan to a file for later apply
Load variable values from a .tfvars file
Check for drift without planning changes

examples.description: Plan with a variables file and save output

commands.name: terraform apply
commands.summary: Apply the changes required to reach the desired state

options.description: Apply a previously saved plan file

examples.description: Apply a saved plan (safe, no surprises)

commands.note: Always review `terraform plan` output before running apply in production.

commands.name: terraform destroy
commands.summary: Destroy all resources managed by this Terraform configuration
commands.note: This permanently deletes infrastructure. Always double-check the plan output.

commands.name: terraform state
commands.summary: Manage the Terraform state file — list, show, move, remove resources

options.description: Show attributes of a resource in state
Remove a resource from state (without destroying it)
Fetch and print the remote state

examples.description: List all resources Terraform knows about
Move resource to a new address after a rename
Remove a resource from state without destroying it

commands.note: Always back up state before running state mv or state rm commands.

commands.name: terraform import
commands.summary: Import existing infrastructure into Terraform state

examples.description: Import an existing AWS instance into Terraform state

categories.name: Inspection & Debugging
categories.description: Validate configs, inspect values, and diagnose issues

commands.name: terraform validate
commands.summary: Check configuration files for syntax errors and internal consistency

options.description: Output validation results as JSON (for CI)

examples.description: Validate all .tf files in current directory

commands.name: terraform output
commands.summary: Read output values from the Terraform state

options.description: Output a single raw string value

examples.description: Get a specific output value (e.g. load balancer DNS)
Get all outputs as JSON for scripting

commands.name: terraform console
commands.summary: Interactive console for evaluating Terraform expressions
